telegraph_text: USS Abraham Lincoln carrier strike group arrives in the CENTCOM area, marking the start of a major US military buildup near Iran.
US orders the carrier USS Gerald R. Ford toward the Middle East, creating a two-carrier posture as Trump pressures Tehran over its nuclear program.
Khamenei rejects limits on enrichment and missiles and warns that US warships in the Gulf could be sunk, as a second talks round opens in Geneva.
USS Gerald R. Ford is sighted off Gibraltar transiting the western Mediterranean en route toward the Middle East theater.
US and Israel launch coordinated airstrikes on Iranian targets; Trump confirms major operations and Netanyahu frames it as removing an existential threat.
Iran's IRGC declares the Strait of Hormuz closed to US- and Israel-allied shipping and begins attacking vessels attempting to transit.
Trump threatens to obliterate Iran's power plants, biggest first, unless Iran fully reopens the Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours.
US and Iran agree to a two-week ceasefire with safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz, brokered by Pakistan.
After the Islamabad talks collapse, the US imposes a naval blockade on Iranian ports and shipping to force the strait back open.
Trump claims the naval blockade is costing Iran about 500 million dollars a day while keeping the blockade in place pending talks.
US and Iranian forces exchange fire in the Strait of Hormuz, with US destroyers reportedly attacked by Iranian missiles, drones and small boats.
US and Iran signal progress toward a 60-day memorandum of understanding that would pause hostilities and reopen the strait.
US conducts fresh strikes in Iran amid stalled talks, reviving fears over the Strait of Hormuz even as a tentative deal is discussed.
After a US helicopter is downed and Iran strikes US bases, Trump says Iran has taken too long and will pay the price as the US resumes strikes.
Trump announces at the G7 that the US and Iran have signed a memorandum of understanding to end the blockade and reopen the Strait of Hormuz toll-free for 60 days.
